title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
After hotel staff confirm they have completed their search of the room, Poirot discusses the physical description of Jenny Hobbs, noting she is approximately forty years old with light yellow hair, a brown hat, and a light brown coat. Poirot realizes this description matches a woman he met at the "Joyful Coffee Shop" the previous Thursday. To investigate further, he decides to re-interview employees, including waiter Rafael Puccini, Thomas Brine, and John Good, to determine how many times they had seen the previous victims, Harriet Sippey, Richard Nigges, and Edith Gransbury. 

Carter informs Poirot that he observed a woman in a light brown coat in the hotel garden being held by Thomas Brine in a peculiar, limp posture. While Carter works on a crossword puzzle, Poirot challenges Carter's defense of Brine. Poirot suggests alternative suspects and methods for moving the body, proposing that waiter Rafael Barker might have used a laundry cart to transport the body through the grand hall, or that Henry Nigges might have used a large suitcase. Poirot also reiterates that the bloodstains indicate the body was dragged toward the door.

Poirot then recounts his findings from London, mentioning conversations with Rafael Borgia, Samuel Kid, Nancy Duane, and Louisa Wallace. However, he withholds specific details regarding a maid named Dolores, a mysterious painting involving Stanley Bill, and the connection between the murder at the Bluxton Hotel and Jenny Hobbs's outburst at the cafe. The discussion is interrupted by Blanche Enderby, who announces that an anxious, tearful woman named Nancy Drew has arrived to see Poirot.
